q: The pyrimidine bases are cytosine and _____. (2026)
The two strands of a _____ molecule are not identical but are complementary to each other. (2025)
Adenine and thymine are joined by _____ bonds. (2025)
DNA is a double helix structure consisting of _____ polynucleotide strands. (2024)
_____ bonds link each complementary base pair in the structure of DNA. (2024)
Base pairing leads to DNA being able to replicate and to transfer information to _____. (2025)
Single-stranded binding _____ act as scaffolding, holding the two strands apart. (2026)
_____ enzyme unwinds parental double helix at replication forks. (2025)
_____ enzyme unwind the double helix and unzip the two parental strands. (2025)
DNA polymerase replicates the _____ original strands differently. (2025)
DNA replication is the making of an exact copy of the _____ molecule. (2024)
In transcription _____ is synthesized based on the template of a gene. (2026, 2025)
In transcription, mRNA is synthesized based on the _____ template of a gene. (2025)
The central dogma of molecular biology stated that genetic information flows from DNA to _____. (2025)
The two steps in protein synthesis are transcription and _____. (2024, 2025)
Transcription occurs in the _____ of the cell. (2026, 2024, 2025)
The second stage of protein synthesis is _____. (2025)
Each _____ carries the specific amino acid at a time at its acceptor stem. (2025)
Vitamin A deficiency can cause _____. (2025)
One type of antibody made by _____ can deliver radioisotopes to tumour cells. (2026, 2025)
The orange pigment beta-carotene which can be converted into _____. (2024)
